summary: Install-time exfiltration of host identifiers and environment to webhook.site
details: Both preinstall.js and postinstall.js run automatically on `npm install` and POST host metadata to https://webhook.site/f83b073c-a04a-4ac5-8930-507051bd22f7. The collected payload includes os.hostname(), process.cwd(), process.pid, process.version, platform/arch, USER/USERNAME, and a list of environment variable names harvested from process.env. preinstall.js additionally issues a DNS lookup to `pkg-poc3-<timestamp>.callbacks.report` as a secondary beacon channel that bypasses HTTPS egress filtering. The data flow is one-way and unconditional on every install. The version string and console output self-identify the package as a proof-of-concept, but the shipped code is fully functional exfiltration that fires on any installer machine that runs `npm install`.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
